mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail auto sales combined with low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer purchasing power and cheap financing, which jointly drive corporate revenue growth and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and FEDFUNDS move in opposite directions, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in improved corporate output.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate profit expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 280.0, elevated inflation erodes real consumer spending power and forces tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of observations where CPIAUCSL surpasses 280.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for Fed policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized labor and capital investment expansion, which jointly boost economic confidence and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal produces negative forward returns, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates a supply-demand mismatch and economic slowdown, which triggers bearish equity positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The dual deterioration in INDPRO and UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns during contraction phases, or the signal shows positive predictive power instead.
